note: SYNTHESIZED from ST 1.18.0 source, not captured. populationInjectionPrompts loops i=0..getExtensionPromptMaxDepth() where MAX_INJECTION_DEPTH=10000 (script.js:499, getExtensionPromptMaxDepth script.js:3222-3223 returns MAX with NO clamping) and each step keeps only `prompt.injection_depth === i` (openai.js:813). So a block at depth 10000 (== MAX) matches at i=10000; after the final whole-array reverse (openai.js:864) it lands at the TOP of the chat region. This fixture pins that boundary case, which BOTH ST and RPT inject at the top. ST does NOT clamp out-of-range depths — a depth > MAX matches no `i` and is DROPPED (never injected); RPT instead CLAMPS the index into the conversation region (idx = max(start, min(base-depth, maxIdx)), promptBuilder.ts), so it would inject a > MAX depth at the top where ST drops it. That divergence is enumerated in KNOWN-DIVERGENCES §8; this fixture stays on the convergent MAX value so `expected.chat` is a true ST oracle. Two-message history. Prose is RPT-authored + scrambled.

input.description: One in-chat system injection at depth 10000 (== MAX_INJECTION_DEPTH, the loop boundary ST includes); lands above all chat messages. Both ST and RPT inject here (convergent).

prompts.name: Main
prompts.content: ORP_cap_main: mind the glass, mind the tide.

character.name: Sol
character.description: Character brindle: Sol, a shelf-watcher counting the quorlish swell.

chat.content: ORP_cap_main: mind the glass, mind the tide.
